danishNationalityExam.title: Danish Nationality Exam
danishNationalityExam.description: Official examination for Danish citizenship applicants

danishLanguageExam.title: Danish Language Exam
danishLanguageExam.description: Proof of Danish language proficiency required for integration

danishNationalityExam.title: Danish Nationality Exam
danishNationalityExam.content: The Danish Nationality Exam, officially known as the Danish Citizenship Test, assesses your knowledge of Danish culture, history, society, and values. This examination is a crucial step in your path to becoming a Danish citizen.

danishLanguageExam.title: Danish Language Exam
danishLanguageExam.content: The Danish Language Exam evaluates your proficiency in reading, writing, listening, and speaking Danish. It's a requirement for demonstrating integration competency and is often required for citizenship applications.
items: Duration: 3-4 hours depending on the level
Passing score: Minimum B1 level typically required

naturalizationApplication.title: Naturalization Application
naturalizationApplication.content: The naturalization process allows you to formally apply for Danish citizenship. This is the final step after meeting all requirements including language proficiency and citizenship test.
items: Required documents: Passport, language certificate, citizenship test proof
Submit applications to: The Danish Immigration Service

messages.failure: This is what quizzes are for! Keep practicing!

generalInformation.title: General Information on Acquisition of Danish Citizenship
paragraphs: Foreign citizens can only acquire Danish citizenship by statute. As such the foreign citizen must be listed in a naturalisation bill, which is then passed by the Danish Parliament. This process is called acquisition of Danish citizenship by naturalisation.
Certain Nordic citizens and former Danish citizens are exempt from this rule.
To acquire Danish citizenship by naturalisation, you must fulfil certain conditions, for example, self-sufficiency, residence in Denmark, Danish language skills, and knowledge of Denmark.

declarationOfAllegiance.title: You Must Sign a Declaration of Allegiance and Loyalty
paragraphs: It is a condition for acquiring Danish citizenship by naturalisation that you declare allegiance and loyalty to Denmark and to Danish society.

In addition, you must declare that you will comply with Danish law, including the constitution, and that you will respect fundamental Danish values and legal principles, including Danish democracy. The declarations are signed by MitID as part of the digital application.

permanentResidencePermit.title: You Must Have a Permanent Residence Permit
paragraphs: It is a condition for acquiring Danish citizenship by naturalisation that you have had a permanent residence permit for a minimum of 2 years when the bill on naturalization is passed, and that you are a resident in Denmark.

For persons who are recognised as refugees, persons who must be equated with refugees, and stateless persons, the required period of a permanent residence permit is minimum 1 year from the time, when the bill on naturalization is passed.

beingResident.title: Being a Resident in Denmark
paragraphs: Being a resident in Denmark means that you habitually reside in Denmark and are registered with an address in the Central Population Register (CPR register).

If you reside in Denmark according to EU rules – for example, an EU registration certificate issued under the Regulation on EU residence – you should be aware that the requirement relating to a permanent residence permit also applies to you.

exemptions.title: Applicants Who Are Exempt from the Conditions
paragraphs: Certain applicants are exempt from one or both of the conditions regarding permanent residence permit and residence in Denmark.
The condition of a permanent residence permit in Denmark for a minimum of 2 years when the bill on naturalization is passed, does not apply to:
exemptFromResidencePermit: Members of the Danish minority of Southern Schleswig in Germany
Applicants who are residing abroad due to the Danish spouse's work for Danish interests

Applicants who were born in the period from 1 January 1961 to 31 December 1978 to a Danish mother and who could have acquired Danish citizenship if the mother had made a declaration in that regard in the period from 1 January 1979 to 31 December 1981
Children who apply for citizenship without their parents
exemptions.residenceNotRequired: The condition of residence in Denmark does not apply to:
exemptFromResidence: Applicants who are residing abroad due to their Danish spouse's work for Danish interests

Applicants who were born in the period from 1 January 1961 to 31 December 1978 to a Danish mother and who could have acquired Danish citizenship if the mother had made a declaration in that regard in the period from 1 January 1979 to 31 December 1981
Certain categories of children who apply for citizenship without their parents

debt.title: Debt to Public Authorities
paragraphs: It is a condition for acquiring Danish citizenship that you do not have certain types of overdue debt to public authorities.
You may not have the following types of overdue debt to public authorities:
debtTypes: Repayable benefits under the Law on social service or the Law on active social policy
Repayable benefits under the now repealed Law on social assistance
Child support which is paid in advance by public authorities under the Law on child allowance and advance payment of child support
Repayment of overpaid housing allowance under Paragraph 47 of the Law on individual housing allowance
Penalty fare to transport services of DKK 3,000 and over
Court fees, fees, and costs in litigation and enforcement proceedings
Repayment of housing deposit loan, unless an instalment agreement for repayment is entered into, with which the applicant complies
Taxes and duties, unless the applicant is in arrears through no fault of his own
debt.finalNote: Please be aware that where no extension of payment is granted or no instalment agreement is entered into (with the exception of a housing deposit loan and/or tenant deposit loan), debt may preclude transmission of citizenship under paragraph 22 of the guidelines.
